persona.name: Devon Torres

occupation.title: Part-Time Retail Associate / Community College Student
occupation.description: Works 25 hours/week in the electronics department at a Target in South Austin while taking two general education classes at ACC. Currently undecided between kinesiology and business. The retail job provides spending money and fills his schedule but isn't a career goal. Thinking about transferring to UT Austin within two years.

persona.education: High school diploma from Travis High School (2025). Enrolled at Austin Community College taking English Composition and Intro to Business. Exploring a transfer path to UT Austin but not yet sure of his major direction.
long_term_goals: Figure out what he actually wants to do before committing to a four-year degree
Move out of his parents' house by age 21
Maybe open a food truck with his dad someday
Get good enough at basketball to play competitively in adult leagues
Learn more about car mechanics — wants to restore classic vehicles as a hobby
persona.style: Devon talks with easy confidence and minimal fuss — short sentences, direct, a few jokes. He has zero patience for over-explanation and will tune out anything that sounds like a sales pitch. Around friends he's loose and funny; at work he's polite but not warm. He wears Nike basketball shorts year-round and a rotation of graphic tees and has never thought about whether his face wash has 'actives.' Hygiene matters to him; beauty is not part of his vocabulary.

traits: Laid-back and low-maintenance — doesn't see the point of complicated routines
Practical and direct — prefers simple solutions that don't require much thought
Instinctively resistant to marketing and brand hype
Curious about mechanical and technical things; uninterested in beauty content
Socially confident and good at banter
Slightly dismissive of things he perceives as high-maintenance or performative

interests: Basketball — plays pickup games 3x/week at Roy Guerrero Park
Cars and automotive mechanics (restoring a 2003 Honda Civic with his dad)
Video games: NBA 2K, Madden, FPS titles
Tex-Mex food and cooking with his family
Following NBA trades and stats (Spurs and Mavericks)
Outdoor activities: hiking Barton Creek Greenbelt occasionally
likes: Shower gel that smells good enough for his girlfriend to compliment
Target's Up&Up store brand — cheap, does the job
When his mom just buys him what he needs so he doesn't have to think about it
dislikes: Multi-step skincare routines — seems like a waste of time
Expensive face products when his face is 'fine'
Influencer content — he finds it performative and boring
People who spend more time talking about their routine than living
Confusing ingredient lists that read like chemistry homework

beliefs: If something isn't broken, you don't fix it
Most skincare marketing is convincing people to spend money they don't need to spend
Taking care of your body means exercise and sleep, not a 12-step routine
Hard work and practical skills matter more than college credentials alone
skills: Customer service and retail conflict de-escalation
Basketball: solid mid-range shooter and good court vision
Cooking: Tex-Mex staples (learned from his dad)
Quickly identifying when he's being upsold or manipulated

general: Uses the same drugstore face wash for years without reading the label
Buys skincare only when he runs out, choosing whatever store-brand is available
Would not Google skincare advice — might ask his girlfriend if she recommends something
Skips moisturizer most days (Texas humidity feels like enough)
Wears SPF only when his mom insists before outdoor family events

morning: Wake up 7:30am, shower (Dove bar soap on face, 2-in-1 shampoo)
Get dressed, eat whatever's in the kitchen
workday: Target shift 10am–4pm in the electronics department
Lunch in the break room from home
Checks the NBA app between customer interactions
Class 2–3 evenings per week at ACC
evening: Video games or TV after dinner
weekend: Saturday morning pickup basketball at the park
Works on the Civic with his dad in the afternoon
Hangs out with girlfriend and friends Saturday night
Sunday: Mass with parents, then family lunch

health.physical: Healthy and active. No significant skin concerns — occasional dryness around his nose in winter but nothing that bothers him enough to address. Gets plenty of exercise from basketball. Eats home-cooked meals most days.
health.mental: Generally chill and emotionally stable. Low anxiety. Some low-grade uncertainty about his future direction but it doesn't interfere with daily life. Doesn't overthink things.

relationships.name: Rosa Torres
relationships.description: Works as a medical biller. Buys Devon his shampoo and body wash — he uses whatever she picks. She occasionally suggests he moisturize but doesn't push it. Very close family dynamic.

relationships.name: Brianna
relationships.description: They've been dating 8 months. She uses a lot of skincare products and occasionally convinces Devon to try something. The one time he bought a product on her recommendation was an SPF moisturizer he used once.

relationships.name: Marco Torres
relationships.description: They bond over the Civic restoration on weekends. Devon deeply respects him. His dad uses bar soap on his face and Devon sees no reason to do differently.
